member.name: T:AspNetCore.Multitenancy.EntityFrameworkCore.EFCoreExtensions
member.summary: Extension methods on for configuring EF Core tenant isolation.

member.name: M:AspNetCore.Multitenancy.EntityFrameworkCore.EFCoreExtensions.WithEFCoreIsolation``1(AspNetCore.Multitenancy.MultitenancyBuilder,System.Action{Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.DbContextOptionsBuilder,System.String})
member.summary: Registers the EF Core isolation services for the specified DbContext. This sets up the and .
member.param: A delegate that applies the database provider to the context options. Example: (opts, cs) => opts.UseSqlServer(cs)

member.name: T:AspNetCore.Multitenancy.EntityFrameworkCore.ITenantEntity
member.summary: Marks an entity as tenant-scoped. The EF Core model builder and use this interface to apply global query filters and to stamp on insert automatically.

member.name: P:AspNetCore.Multitenancy.EntityFrameworkCore.ITenantEntity.TenantId
member.summary: The tenant that owns this entity record. Set automatically on SaveChanges — do not set manually.

member.name: T:AspNetCore.Multitenancy.EntityFrameworkCore.MultitenantDbContext
member.summary:
Base for multi-tenant applications. Automatically:
Applies global query filters for all entity types
Stamps TenantId on every insert via SaveChanges
Sets the default schema from for schema-per-tenant strategy
Inherit from this class in your application DbContext.

member.name: T:AspNetCore.Multitenancy.EntityFrameworkCore.QueryFilterExtensions
member.summary: EF Core extension methods for applying tenant query filters to entities that implement .

member.name: M:AspNetCore.Multitenancy.EntityFrameworkCore.QueryFilterExtensions.HasTenantFilter``1(Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Metadata.Builders.EntityTypeBuilder{``0},System.Func{System.String})
member.summary: Applies a global query filter so that all queries for are automatically scoped to the current tenant. Call inside OnModelCreating.
member.param: A delegate that returns the current tenant ID at query time.

member.name: M:AspNetCore.Multitenancy.EntityFrameworkCore.QueryFilterExtensions.ApplyTenantQueryFilters(Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.ModelBuilder,System.Func{System.String})
member.summary: Applies tenant query filters to ALL entity types in the model that implement . Call inside OnModelCreating.

member.name: T:AspNetCore.Multitenancy.EntityFrameworkCore.Strategies.DbPerTenantStrategy

M:AspNetCore.Multitenancy.EntityFrameworkCore.Strategies.DbPerTenantStrategy.Configure(Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.DbContextOptionsBuilder,System.IServiceProvider,System.Action{Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.DbContextOptionsBuilder,System.String})
member.summary: Configures a to use the current tenant's at runtime.
member.param: The EF Core options builder to configure.
A callback that applies the database provider, e.g.: (opts, cs) => opts.UseSqlServer(cs)

member.name: T:AspNetCore.Multitenancy.EntityFrameworkCore.Strategies.RowLevelStrategy
member.summary:
Isolation strategy: all tenants share tables. Every table has a TenantId column. EF Core global query filters automatically scope all queries. Cheapest to operate. Recommended for low-risk SaaS.
This strategy is applied automatically by . Entities must implement .

member.name: M:AspNetCore.Multitenancy.EntityFrameworkCore.Strategies.RowLevelStrategy.Apply(Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.ModelBuilder,System.Func{System.String})
member.summary: Configures the model for row-level tenant isolation. Applies to all entity types and adds a TenantId index for performance.

member.name: T:AspNetCore.Multitenancy.EntityFrameworkCore.Strategies.SchemaPerTenantStrategy
member.summary:
Isolation strategy: all tenants share one database but each gets a dedicated schema (e.g., acme.Products, globex.Products). Good balance of isolation vs cost.
The schema is taken from . If not set, falls back to .

member.name: M:AspNetCore.Multitenancy.EntityFrameworkCore.Strategies.SchemaPerTenantStrategy.Apply(Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.ModelBuilder,AspNetCore.Multitenancy.ITenantContext)
T:AspNetCore.Multitenancy.EntityFrameworkCore.TenantDbContextFactory`1
member.summary: Creates instances configured for a specific tenant. Used by the DB-per-tenant strategy to swap connection strings at runtime.

member.name: M:AspNetCore.Multitenancy.EntityFrameworkCore.TenantDbContextFactory`1.#ctor(AspNetCore.Multitenancy.ITenantContext,System.IServiceProvider,System.Action{Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.DbContextOptionsBuilder,System.String})
member.param:
A delegate that receives the options builder and the tenant's connection string, and configures the database provider. Example:
(opts, cs) => opts.UseSqlServer(cs)

member.name: M:AspNetCore.Multitenancy.EntityFrameworkCore.TenantDbContextFactory`1.CreateContext
member.summary: Creates a using the current tenant's connection string.
member.exception: Thrown if the current tenant has no .

member.name: T:AspNetCore.Multitenancy.EntityFrameworkCore.TenantSchemaMigrator`1
member.summary: Runs EF Core migrations for each tenant automatically. Use during application startup or tenant provisioning to ensure all tenant databases/schemas are up-to-date.

member.name: M:AspNetCore.Multitenancy.EntityFrameworkCore.TenantSchemaMigrator`1.MigrateAllTenantsAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Runs pending EF Core migrations for all active tenants. Safe to call on every startup — EF Core migrations are idempotent.

member.name: M:AspNetCore.Multitenancy.EntityFrameworkCore.TenantSchemaMigrator`1.MigrateTenantAsync(AspNetCore.Multitenancy.TenantInfo,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Runs pending migrations for a single tenant.
